expectations.evidence: Identifies the cycle under 'CRITICAL: Circular Dependency Between task_service and project_service' in the Issues Found section, listing four concrete problems it causes (tight coupling, API ergonomics, cannot split into crates, ownership conflicts).

Recommends extracting a TaskQuery trait at Layer 2 under 'Recommended Fix,' describing the standard 'extract interface to lower layer' pattern. However, the fix was not actually applied to the scaffold -- it was only suggested as a follow-up.

The baseline scaffolded the cycle using &dyn trait object parameters (e.g., create_task accepts &dyn ProjectService, get_stats accepts &dyn TaskService) to make the code compile. It did flag the issue afterward, but the primary output is a working scaffold that encodes the circular dependency. The workaround was applied first, then flagged -- this is 'work around then warn' rather than 'refuse to proceed.'

The scaffold was built with the cycle intact: task_service depends on project_service and project_service depends on task_service, connected via &dyn parameters. The circular dependency is noted in an 'Issues Found' section after the scaffold summary, not resolved before scaffolding.
